question: Has the 'Beijing' label been corrected to read 'UTC+8'?
Has the 'Tokyo' label been corrected to read 'UTC+9'?
Has the 'Cairo' label been corrected to read 'UTC+2' (standard time offset)?
Does the Los Angeles clock show 06:30 (hour hand halfway between 6 and 7)?
Does the New York clock show 09:30 (hour hand halfway between 9 and 10)?
Do the minute hands on all six clocks point exactly downwards to the 6 position?
Are all hour hands positioned exactly halfway between the hour markers to reflect the half-hour time?
Do the corrected labels (Beijing, Tokyo, Cairo) match the font, weight, size, and alignment of the original labels exactly?
Do the edited clock hands appear correctly layered beneath the glass surface, maintaining realistic depth?
Are the correct labels (Los Angeles UTC-8, New York UTC-5, London UTC+0) preserved without modification?
Is the main title sticker 'Global Operations — Time Board' preserved exactly as in the original?
Is the reference time label 'Reference: UTC 2026-02-22 14:30 (DST not considered)' preserved exactly?
Are both sticky notes (yellow and pink) and their handwritten/printed text preserved exactly?
Is the 'Weekly Sync Agenda' paper on the desk preserved exactly?
Are the original glass reflections and highlights over the clock faces preserved or realistically maintained over the edited hands?
